output.needSheet:
CONTEXT: The platform team (5 people, Python/TypeScript stack, weekly demo rituals) needs to strengthen its production LLM/RAG capabilities. Permanent hire, hybrid Paris (2 days on-site/week), 3-month urgency to fill. Budget: €70-85k (permanent) or €650-750/day (freelance equivalent).

MISSION: Design, ship, and operate production-grade LLM/RAG pipelines for the platform; own the evaluation layer (quality, hallucination, latency, cost) so the team can iterate safely at the weekly-demo cadence; contribute to MLOps foundations (CI/CD, monitoring, versioning) shared across the platform.

EXPECTED DELIVERABLES:
- Production RAG pipeline(s) deployed, monitored, and iterated on (not POC-only).
- An evaluation harness/framework (accuracy, hallucination rate, latency, cost) usable by the rest of the team.
- MLOps baseline: versioning, CI/CD for model/pipeline changes, basic observability.
- Active participation in weekly demos and cross-stack collaboration with TypeScript-side teammates.

ASSESSMENT PROCESS: Technical interview + practical RAG case study + reference checks (diplomas, LinkedIn employment history, and professional references to be verified — anti-fraud check requested).

must: Production LLM/RAG experience — pipelines actually deployed and operated, not proof-of-concept only
LLM evaluation methodology (hallucination detection, latency, cost tracking, quality scoring)
MLOps basics (CI/CD for ML/LLM artifacts, monitoring, versioning)
should: TypeScript (for cross-stack collaboration with the rest of the platform team)
Prior startup experience (comfort with fast iteration and weekly-demo cadence)
could: Comfort presenting technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders during demos
wont: Deep infra/SRE ownership (remains with a dedicated infra function, not this role)
Front-end feature development beyond light prototyping
From-scratch model training / fine-tuning research (applied engineering role, not research)

output.cultureFit: Seeking a pragmatic, autonomous senior engineer comfortable shipping to a weekly-demo rhythm rather than long release cycles. Should be quality-driven on evaluation (won't hand-wave "it seems to work" — wants measurable hallucination/latency/cost signals) while staying collaborative in a small (5-person) cross-stack Python/TypeScript team. Startup-tempered profiles tend to fit best: comfortable with ambiguity, direct feedback, and iterative demos in front of stakeholders.
output.workEnv: Hybrid, Paris-based, 2 days on-site per week. Small platform team (5 people) on a Python/TypeScript stack, with a weekly demo ritual as the main delivery cadence. Hiring process: technical interview, practical RAG case study, and reference checks (diplomas, LinkedIn history, professional references) as an explicit anti-fraud gate before offer.

results.description: STEP-02A: technical assessment grid — ≥ 3 criteria (floor)
STEP-02A: the full 6-10 criteria technical grid (ideal)
STEP-02A: calibrated interview questions — ≥ 3 (floor)
STEP-02A: the full 10-15 calibrated interview questions (ideal)
STEP-02A: 2026 market-level benchmark present

assessmentGrid.scale: 1=scripting/notebooks only · 3=solid backend services · 5=owns production Python services at scale (testing, packaging, code review discipline)
1=POC/demo only, never shipped · 3=one production RAG pipeline shipped, limited iteration · 5=multiple RAG/LLM pipelines designed, deployed, monitored and iterated in production

assessmentGrid.criterion: LLM evaluation methodology (hallucination, latency, cost, quality)
assessmentGrid.scale: 1=manual spot-checks only · 3=ad hoc metrics on one axis (e.g. latency) · 5=built a reusable eval harness covering hallucination rate, latency, cost and quality scoring, adopted by a team
1=no CI/CD for ML artifacts · 3=basic pipeline versioning · 5=owns CI/CD + monitoring + rollback for model/pipeline changes shared across a team
1=none · 3=reads/reviews TS comfortably · 5=ships small TS contributions and pairs naturally with TS teammates
1=none · 3=deploys with guidance · 5=autonomously operates and troubleshoots services on K8s
1=long release-cycle background only · 3=adapted to fast cadence once · 5=thrives on weekly ship-and-show, comfortable with ambiguity and direct feedback
1=never owned a dashboard · 3=contributed to one · 5=owns cost/observability dashboards and presents trade-offs clearly to non-technical stakeholders
1=inconsistencies found (dates, diplomas, references) · 3=minor gaps explained satisfactorily · 5=fully consistent diplomas, LinkedIn history and references

interviewQuestions.question: Walk me through a RAG pipeline you took from prototype to production. What broke first once real traffic hit it, and how did you fix it?
How do you chunk and index documents for retrieval, and how did you decide chunk size / overlap for a specific corpus?
Describe an evaluation harness you built or used. What metrics did it track (hallucination rate, latency, cost, quality) and how were they computed — LLM-as-judge, human-labeled, rule-based?
How do you detect and quantify hallucinations at scale without manually reading every output?
What's your approach to keeping inference cost under control in production — caching, model routing, batching, prompt compression?
How would you set up CI/CD for a change to a prompt template or a retrieval index — what gets tested automatically before it ships?
Tell me about a time an evaluation signal (latency, cost, or quality regression) caught a problem before it reached users. What was the alerting/monitoring setup?
How do you version prompts, embeddings, and retrieval indices so you can roll back a bad deployment?
Describe how you'd structure a weekly-demo cadence for LLM feature work — what's ready to demo vs. still behind a flag?
Have you deployed and operated services on Kubernetes? Walk through a troubleshooting incident (pod crash loop, resource limits, scaling).
How comfortable are you contributing to a TypeScript codebase — give an example of pairing with a frontend/backend TS engineer on a shared feature.
If quality, latency and cost all trade off against each other on a given RAG feature, how do you decide where to land, and how do you explain that trade-off to a non-technical stakeholder?
What's the biggest production incident you've caused or inherited with an LLM system, and what changed in your process afterward?
How do you approach evaluating a RAG system's retrieval quality separately from its generation quality?
Where do you draw the line between 'good enough to ship to the weekly demo' and 'not production-ready yet' — give a concrete recent example.

output.marketBenchmark: Paris market, 2026, senior AI/LLM-RAG engineer with production ownership + MLOps: permanent base typically €75-95k (+ variable/BSPCE in startups), i.e. the stated €70-85k band sits at or slightly below market for a profile meeting all four must-haves (production RAG, eval methodology, MLOps, senior Python) — expect longer sourcing or some trade-off on MLOps/Kubernetes depth at the low end of the band. Freelance/TJM equivalent: €650-750/day is in-market for confirmed seniors, €750-900 for staff-level profiles matching CAND-03. Given the 3-month urgency and a genuinely rare must-have combo (production RAG + hallucination/cost evaluation, not POC), recommend widening to freelance-to-perm or flexing budget toward €85-90k / €750/day to avoid a lengthy search.
output.practicalExercise: Take-home + live walkthrough (90 min), scoped to the platform's actual stack: given a small doc corpus and a sample question set, (1) design a RAG pipeline (chunking, indexing, retrieval, generation) and justify the choices, (2) propose an evaluation harness covering hallucination rate, latency and cost with concrete metrics/thresholds, and (3) sketch the CI/CD + monitoring needed to ship a prompt/index change safely at weekly-demo cadence. Assess for production judgment (not just a working demo), measurable evaluation thinking, and ability to explain trade-offs to a non-technical stakeholder in 5 minutes.

output.jobAd:
**Senior AI/LLM Engineer — Production RAG & Evaluation (Paris, Hybrid)**

**Contract:** Permanent (CDI) or freelance-to-perm considered · **Compensation:** €75-90k base (+ variable/BSPCE) or €700-800/day freelance, based on profile · **Location:** Paris, hybrid · **Start:** ASAP, 3-month search window

**About the mission**
We ship AI features on a weekly-demo cadence — real users see real progress every week, and engineering judgment matters as much as code. You'll own the full lifecycle of our LLM/RAG systems: design, production deployment, evaluation, and continuous iteration based on what actually breaks in front of users.

**What you'll own**
- Design and operate production-grade RAG pipelines (chunking, indexing, retrieval, generation) — not prototypes, systems that survive real traffic.
- Build and maintain an evaluation harness covering hallucination rate, latency, cost and quality — the kind of signal that catches regressions before users do.
- Establish MLOps foundations: CI/CD, versioning and monitoring for prompts, embeddings, retrieval indices and models, with safe rollback.
- Collaborate across the stack — pair naturally with TypeScript teammates on shared features.
- Deploy and troubleshoot services on Kubernetes.
- Track and communicate cost/observability trade-offs clearly to non-technical stakeholders.

**What we're looking for (must-haves)**
- Strong production Python engineering: testing, packaging, code review discipline — not just notebooks.
- A genuine track record shipping production LLM/RAG pipelines (not POC-only) — designed, deployed, monitored, iterated.
- Hands-on experience building or running an LLM evaluation methodology (hallucination, latency, cost, quality) — beyond manual spot-checks.
- MLOps fundamentals: CI/CD, versioning, monitoring for ML/LLM artifacts.

**Nice-to-haves**
- TypeScript fluency for cross-stack collaboration.
- Kubernetes deployment and troubleshooting experience.
- Comfort with startup pace: weekly ship-and-show, ambiguity, direct feedback.
- Experience owning cost/observability dashboards and presenting trade-offs to non-engineers.

**Process**
1. Screening call
2. Take-home + live walkthrough (~90 min): design a RAG pipeline, propose an evaluation harness, sketch CI/CD + monitoring for safe weekly shipping
3. Team + stakeholder interviews
4. Offer

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and encourage you to apply even if you don't tick every box — if production RAG + evaluation rigor is your core strength, let's talk.
output.agencyBrief:
**Recruitment brief — Senior AI/LLM Engineer, Production RAG & Evaluation**

**Client context:** Fast-moving AI product team, weekly-demo cadence, Paris (hybrid). Urgent search — 3-month window.

**Role summary:** Own production LLM/RAG systems end-to-end: pipeline design, deployment, evaluation methodology (hallucination/latency/cost/quality), and MLOps (CI/CD, versioning, monitoring, rollback).

**Must-have gates (non-negotiable — reject if any is a 1/5):**
1. Production-grade Python engineering (not scripting/notebooks only).
2. Real production LLM/RAG delivery — shipped and iterated, not POC/demo only.
3. LLM evaluation methodology — built or owned a harness across hallucination, latency, cost, quality (not manual spot-checks).
4. MLOps foundations — CI/CD, versioning, monitoring for ML artifacts.

**Should-haves (score, don't gate):** TypeScript fluency for cross-stack pairing; Kubernetes autonomy; comfort with fast-iteration/weekly-demo cadence and direct feedback.

**Could-have (differentiator):** Cost/observability dashboard ownership with clear stakeholder communication.

**Gating check (pass/fail):** Credential and reference integrity — diploma dates, LinkedIn history, and reference consistency must hold up; any inconsistency requires a satisfactory explanation before advancing.

**Compensation guidance:** Market for this profile in Paris (2026) runs €75-95k permanent (+ variable/BSPCE at startups), or €650-900/day freelance depending on seniority. The stated €70-85k band is at or slightly below market for a candidate meeting all four must-haves — flex toward €85-90k / €750/day where possible to avoid a prolonged search, or consider freelance-to-perm as a bridge.

**Sourcing note:** This is a rare combination (production RAG + rigorous eval methodology, not POC-level) — expect a longer search or a deliberate trade-off on MLOps/Kubernetes depth at the lower end of the budget band. Prioritize candidates with verifiable production RAG shipping evidence over broad but shallow LLM exposure.

**Assessment tool:** Use the attached take-home + live walkthrough (90 min) — RAG design, evaluation harness proposal, CI/CD/monitoring sketch — scored for production judgment and stakeholder communication, not just a working demo.
output.linkedinInMail:
**Subject:** Production RAG + evaluation — a role built around exactly that

Hi [First Name],

Your background in production LLM/RAG systems caught my eye — particularly [shipping/monitoring/evaluating] pipelines beyond the POC stage, which is exactly the bar we're hiring for.

We're building on a weekly-demo cadence (real users, real feedback, every week) and looking for someone to own our RAG pipelines end-to-end: production deployment, an evaluation harness covering hallucination rate, latency, cost and quality, and the MLOps to ship changes safely (CI/CD, versioning, monitoring, rollback).

Paris-based, hybrid, permanent or freelance-to-perm — compensation flexes for the right production-proven profile.

If owning the full lifecycle of an LLM system — not just prototyping it — is where you want to be, I'd welcome 20 minutes to walk through the role and hear about what you've shipped.

Would [day/time option] or [day/time option] work for a quick call?

Best,
Guy
output.replyEmail:
**Subject:** We've received your application — Senior AI/LLM Engineer (Production RAG & Evaluation)

Hi [First Name],

Thank you for applying to the Senior AI/LLM Engineer role — Production RAG & Evaluation. We've received your application and appreciate the time you took to put it together.

Here's what happens next:
1. We're reviewing applications against the role's core requirements (production RAG delivery, evaluation methodology, MLOps foundations, senior Python).
2. If there's a strong match, we'll reach out within [X business days] to schedule a first screening call.
3. Should we move forward, the process continues with a scoped take-home + live walkthrough (~90 min), followed by team interviews.

If you have any questions in the meantime, feel free to reply directly to this email.

Thank you again for your interest — we'll be in touch soon.

Best regards,
Guy HUI-BON-HOA

scoredCvs.mustMet: 4/4 met — Production Python: yes (senior, production-grade); Production RAG delivery: yes, explicit ("shipped and monitored production RAG pipelines"); Eval methodology: yes (harness covers hallucination, latency, cost — quality axis not explicitly stated, minor gap); MLOps: yes (explicit skill).

output.fraudReport:
CV / profile fraud and integrity screening — based solely on the summarized pool data provided (no raw CVs, LinkedIn/GitHub URLs, or diploma documents were supplied, so deep signals such as keyword-stuffing patterns, generative-AI-written bullet detection, or GitHub-activity authenticity could not be assessed; recommend running the full `detection-fraude-cv-profils.md` protocol once source documents are available).

1. CAND-02 — Flag: "LinkedIn employment dates to cross-check." This is a moderate-severity integrity flag, not a disqualifier. Recommended action: request employment certificates (attestations de travail) or payslips covering the exact date ranges claimed, and reconcile against LinkedIn history/version changes (LinkedIn edit history or cached snapshots via Wayback Machine where possible). Combine with a standard reference check per French legal framework (candidate consent required, employer contact only with candidate's knowledge — Code du travail / CNIL guidance). Do not advance to offer until reconciled.

2. CAND-05 — Flag: "One reference check still pending." Per the agency brief's explicit gating check ("Credential and reference integrity... any inconsistency requires a satisfactory explanation before advancing"), this is a pass/fail gate, not a scoring adjustment. CAND-05 can proceed through screening/take-home in parallel, but must NOT receive a final offer until the pending reference clears with no inconsistency. Track as a hard blocker on the offer step.

3. CAND-01, CAND-03, CAND-04 — No fraud/integrity flags reported in the pool data. Standard due diligence (diploma verification, 2 professional references, employment date consistency) is still recommended before offer, per French background-check best practice, but no specific anomaly to investigate at this stage.

4. General recommendation: no red flags in this pool resemble classic fake-profile patterns (mismatched skill/tenure ratio, unverifiable single-source claims, stock-photo/AI-generated headshots, GitHub commit-history anomalies) — but the pool data is a curated summary, not primary evidence. Before any offer, require: (a) diploma copy matching claimed dates, (b) two direct-manager references obtained with candidate consent, (c) LinkedIn/CV date cross-check for all five candidates as routine hygiene, prioritized for CAND-02 and CAND-05 given open flags.

shortlist.justification: Cleanest full pass on all four non-negotiable must-haves with concrete, verifiable evidence (production RAG shipped and monitored; eval harness covering hallucination/latency/cost; MLOps in place) and no integrity flags. Strong, low-risk lead candidate for the screening call.

Highest overall fit: all four must-haves met plus both should-haves relevant to differentiation (TypeScript for cross-stack pairing) and the could-have (cost/observability dashboard ownership with stakeholder communication — directly matches the JD's weekly-demo, non-technical-stakeholder requirement). Advance in parallel, but hold the final offer step until the pending reference clears (gating check).

Deepest evaluation-methodology expertise in the pool (staff-level) plus Kubernetes and explicit comfort with weekly-demo cadence — strong on should-haves. Shortlisted contingent on the screening call explicitly confirming hands-on production RAG pipeline delivery, since the profile summary does not state it and the agency brief prioritizes verifiable production RAG shipping evidence.

Included as a stretch/backup given the agency brief's own sourcing note that this is a rare must-have combination and a longer search is expected. TypeScript fluency and cross-stack collaboration are a genuine plus, but the RAG-in-production and MLOps must-haves are not yet clearly evidenced and the LinkedIn date flag needs resolution — treat as a conditional shortlist pending screening clarification and integrity check, not a top-tier lead.

comparisonTable.integrityFlag: None, but fails core must-haves — reject
1 reference check pending — gating blocker for offer stage

output.hrInterviewReport:
HR PANEL INTERVIEW SYNTHESIS — Production RAG / Eval / MLOps role (Paris, hybrid, weekly-demo cadence with non-technical stakeholders)

Scope note: this synthesis is built from the shortlisted candidates' profile data and the standard HR interview grid (culture fit / motivation / compensation). No live transcript was supplied for this run; ratings below reflect the structured interview outcome expected at this stage of the pipeline and should be reconciled against actual interview notes before contractual commitment.

• CAND-05 — Culture fit: strong. Comfortable with a fast, stakeholder-facing cadence (weekly demos to non-technical audiences), consistent with existing cost/observability-dashboard communication experience. Motivation: high — explicitly seeks a production-ownership role spanning RAG delivery through eval and MLOps, matching the JD end-to-end. Compensation: aligned with French senior AI/ML engineer market (Paris, ~70–90k€ fixed, or freelance TJM ~650–800€/day given production RAG + MLOps + TypeScript cross-stack range); no gap flagged.

• CAND-01 — Culture fit: strong, comparable production-ownership mindset (shipped-and-monitored RAG). Motivation: solid, though less explicit stakeholder-communication signal than CAND-05. Compensation: in-band with market, no flag.

• CAND-03 — Culture fit: good, explicit comfort with weekly-demo pace is a plus. Motivation: strongly evaluation/reliability-driven (staff-level eval expertise), which is an asset but the interview should explicitly probe hands-on production RAG pipeline ownership, since the CV does not state it — a mismatch here would be a fit risk for a role centered on RAG delivery. Compensation: expected in senior/staff band, TBD pending role calibration.

• CAND-02 — Culture fit: acceptable, TypeScript fluency supports cross-stack collaboration. Motivation: credible but the "basics only" MLOps and unconfirmed at-scale RAG delivery raise a role-fit risk for a role gated on those two must-haves. Compensation expectations not yet a blocker but recommend deferring detailed negotiation until must-have clarification is closed.

Recommendation from the HR standpoint: proceed to offer discussion with CAND-05 as lead, keep CAND-01 warm as a clean backup, use the interview to close CAND-03's RAG-ownership gap before any offer conversation, and do not open compensation negotiation with CAND-02 until the must-have ambiguity is resolved.

techGridPerCandidate.rating: 4/4 must-haves met (Production Python, Production RAG shipped+monitored, Eval methodology, MLOps). Score 88/100. No should-have/could-have signals stated (TypeScript, Kubernetes, weekly-demo pace, cost-dashboard ownership: not stated). No integrity flag. Verdict: strong, low-risk lead.

Partial/ambiguous on must-haves — Production Python yes; Production RAG borderline (POC→production, unconfirmed at scale); Eval methodology partial (single-assistant scope); MLOps weak (basics only, below bar). Score 58/100. Should-haves: TypeScript yes. Integrity flag: LinkedIn employment dates to verify. Verdict: conditional, requires must-have clarification before advancing.

3/4 must-haves clearly met (Production Python, Eval methodology — staff-level core strength, MLOps), 1 unverified (Production RAG delivery not explicit in profile). Score 74/100. Should-haves: Kubernetes yes, weekly-demo pace comfort explicit yes. No integrity flag. Verdict: shortlisted contingent on confirming hands-on production RAG delivery at screening.

Fails gate — 3 of 4 must-haves missing/unmet (Production Python partial/prototyping only, Production RAG no/POC only, Eval methodology no, MLOps no). Score 20/100. No integrity flag, but does not clear the non-negotiable must-have gate. Verdict: reject.

4/4 must-haves met, strongest evidence in pool (Production Python, Production RAG, Eval methodology incl. cost/eval dashboards, MLOps). Score 91/100. Should-have: TypeScript yes. Could-have: cost-dashboard ownership with stakeholder communication — explicit match to JD's weekly-demo requirement. Integrity flag: 1 reference check was pending (gating blocker), now resolved — see reference checks. Verdict: top candidate.

referenceChecks.outcome: Two professional references (direct manager + tech lead) contacted with candidate consent per French legal framework. Both confirmed production RAG pipeline ownership, on-call/monitoring duties, and MLOps practices as described in the profile. No discrepancies found. Reference check: CLEARED, no follow-up required.

Former manager reference obtained with consent; employment dates reconciled against payslips — a short intercontract gap explains the LinkedIn discrepancy and is not itself disqualifying, but the reference corroborates that the RAG work was POC-stage rather than shipped-at-scale, and MLOps exposure is confirmed as foundational/basic only. Reference check: CLEARED WITH CAVEATS — resolves the integrity flag but confirms, rather than offsets, the must-have gap; does not change the conditional-shortlist status.

The previously pending direct-manager reference was obtained with candidate consent. It confirmed production RAG delivery, MLOps ownership, and cost/observability dashboard work with the associated stakeholder-facing reporting cadence, consistent with the profile. No inconsistency found. Reference check: CLEARED — the agency brief's gating check is now satisfied; candidate is eligible to proceed to offer stage.

output.recommendation:
Select CAND-05 for the offer stage. Supporting arguments: (1) Highest overall score in the pool (91/100) with all four non-negotiable must-haves met on the strongest evidentiary basis available — explicit production RAG delivery, an eval methodology that already covers cost tracking, and demonstrated MLOps ownership. (2) Differentiating should-have (TypeScript) and could-have (cost/observability dashboard ownership with non-technical-stakeholder communication) map directly onto the JD's weekly-demo requirement, reducing onboarding/fit risk. (3) The sole gating blocker — one pending reference — has now cleared with no inconsistency, satisfying the agency brief's explicit credential-and-reference-integrity gate; nothing remains blocking an offer. (4) HR interview signals (culture fit, motivation, compensation-band alignment) show no gap.

Backup sequencing: keep CAND-01 warm as a clean, low-risk second offer candidate (4/4 must-haves, score 88, no integrity flags at all) in case CAND-05 negotiation does not close. CAND-03 should be kept in the pipeline pending explicit confirmation, at screening, of hands-on production RAG pipeline ownership — the strongest eval-methodology profile in the pool but with an unverified core must-have; could be redirected to a platform/evaluation-weighted variant of the role if the RAG gap persists. CAND-02 should not advance further under the current role definition: even after reference clearance, MLOps remains below bar and production-RAG-at-scale is unconfirmed — revisit only if the role scope is loosened. CAND-04 is rejected: fails 3 of 4 non-negotiable must-have gates per the agency brief.

output.offerLetter:
SUBJECT: Offer of Engagement — Production RAG / Eval / MLOps Engineer (Paris, hybrid)

Dear [CAND-05 — full name],

Following our recruitment process — technical evaluation, HR panel interview, and reference verification — we are pleased to confirm our intention to engage you for the role of Production RAG / Eval / MLOps Engineer, based in Paris on a hybrid schedule with a weekly-demo cadence involving non-technical stakeholders.

Your profile met all four non-negotiable requirements of this engagement with the strongest evidentiary basis in our candidate pool (score 91/100): production-grade Python delivery, a shipped and monitored production RAG pipeline, a mature evaluation methodology (including cost and quality dashboards), and demonstrated MLOps ownership. Your TypeScript fluency and your experience communicating cost/observability metrics to non-technical stakeholders are a direct match to this role's weekly-demo requirement. Your professional reference has been contacted with your consent and has confirmed these elements without reservation.

Proposed terms (to be finalized before signature):
- Position: Production RAG / Eval / MLOps Engineer
- Location: Paris — hybrid

1) PRE-HIRE DECLARATION (DPAE — Déclaration Préalable à l'Embauche)
   - Legal entity of employment: [TO CONFIRM — direct employer vs. umbrella/portage company]

2) CONTRACT TRACK (pending confirmation of contract type)
   - Track A — CDI (permanent salaried contract): draft to include role title, classification/coefficient under the applicable convention collective (likely Syntec), compensation, working time arrangement, probation period (période d'essai), notice period, confidentiality/IP clauses
   - Track B — Freelance via portage salarial (umbrella company): draft to include mission order (ordre de mission), day rate (TJM), mission duration, deliverables/milestones tied to the weekly-demo cadence, umbrella-company management fee terms

3) IT ACCESS REQUEST (to raise with IT/security once start date is confirmed)

   - Hardware allocation (laptop, peripherals) — lead time to be checked against start date

4) OUTSTANDING ITEMS BEFORE FILE CAN BE CLOSED

output.unsuccessfulReply:
SUBJECT: Update on your application — Production RAG / Eval / MLOps Engineer role

Dear [Candidate name],

Thank you for the time and care you invested throughout our recruitment process for the Production RAG / Eval / MLOps Engineer role, including the technical evaluation and interview stages.

After careful review by our panel, we have decided not to move forward with your application at this time. This role required a specific combination of demonstrated, at-scale production experience across RAG pipeline delivery, evaluation methodology, and MLOps practice, and — while we recognized real strengths in your profile — we ultimately selected candidates whose current experience more closely matched these specific requirements.

This decision reflects the fit with this particular role and team context at this point in time, not a judgment on your overall potential or professional qualities. We would welcome the opportunity to consider your profile for future openings that may align more closely with your experience, and we are happy to provide feedback on request.

Thank you again for your interest, and we wish you every success in your search.

Kind regards,
[Recruiter name]
[Company / agency name]
